student_message: Oh thank god. I was so scared. I think we are done now... but I'm probably wrong. Sorry, did I mess up?
final_message:
Math can be hard sometimes, Kabir, and it is totally okay to feel a little scared! You did not mess up at all, so please do not apologize. 💛 You did an amazing job noticing that the sides do not match when you fold it. Remember, making mistakes is just a normal part of learning, and I am so proud of you! 

We are almost done with our fun shapes. Shall we look at just one more shape together to see if we can find its perfect fold line, or line of symmetry? Take a deep breath, you are doing wonderfully! 🌟

orchestrator_intent.goal: Reassure Kabir he did not mess up and gently guide him to the next step.
orchestrator_intent.reasoning: Kabir is expressing high fear and anxiety about making mistakes. The dialogue agent will answer his question while the encouragement agent provides necessary emotional support.

current_section_title: Reflection Symmetry and Lines of Symmetry
current_section_content: A line of symmetry is a fold line that divides a shape into two halves that are perfect mirror images of each other. Some shapes have a vertical line of symmetry (left-right mirror), some have a horizontal line (top-bottom mirror), and some have both. You can use this idea to cut out shapes by drawing only half or one-quarter of the shape on folded paper, then cutting and unfolding. Letters like A have one vertical line of symmetry, while letters like H have both vertical and horizontal lines of symmetry.
session_summary: The session covered vertical and horizontal lines of symmetry. Kabir successfully identifies these symmetries, accurately recognizing vertical lines. However, he struggles with severe math anxiety, extreme self-doubt, and a fear of making mistakes, repeatedly apologizing despite providing correct answers. Currently, Kabir grasps the foundational math concepts well but severely lacks confidence. His learning state remains heavily dominated by anxiety, requiring continuous emotional support and reassurance to participate and progress.
